texto: Well, we have to do something. There are all sorts of rumours about soldiers coming up.""These people are full of rumours. They love rumours." Paterson stood watching the bridge. "Their whole life is a rumour.

frase_original: Well, we have to do something. There are all sorts of rumours about soldiers coming up.""These people are full of rumours. They love rumours." Paterson stood watching the bridge. "Their whole life is a rumour.

texto: Miss Parkinson lived alone in a big bay-windowed house of Edwardian brick with a vast garden of decaying fruit trees and untidy hedges of gigantic size. She was great at making elderberry wine and bottling fruit and preserves and lemon curd and drying flowers for winter. She felt, like Halibut, that things were not as they used to be. The synthetic curse of modern times lay thick on everything. There was everywhere a sad drift from Nature.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a H.E. Bates: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Miss Parkinson lived alone in a big bay-windowed house of Edwardian brick with a vast garden of d…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, descriptive, dreamy pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dreamy e garden.

texto: All day, after two days and nights of rain, water had been rising in the dykes and now it was creeping rapidly up the five stone arches of the bridge where the she stood watching the wide rainy valley up which the tongue of river finally lost itself in a gray country of winter elms.

texto: Every morning Mrs Eglantine sat at the round bamboo bar of the New Pacific Hotel and drank her breakfast. This consisted of two quick large brandies, followed by several slower ones. By noon breakfast had become lunch and by two o'clock the pouches under and above Mrs Eglantine's bleared blue eyes began to look like large puffed pink prawns.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a H.E. Bates: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao.
